cards.back: A data structure used in operating systems to store the physical addresses of pages in memory, along with their corresponding virtual page numbers and other relevant information.
A contiguous block of memory that is managed by the operating system, containing a page of virtual memory and its corresponding physical address.
A small cache used in processors to store recently used translation entries, which helps to speed up page table lookups.
A single entry in the page table, containing information such as the virtual page number, physical frame number, and other relevant data.
An algorithm used to select a page to replace when the system is running low on memory, such as First-In-First-Out (FIFO), Least Recently Used (LRU), and Optimal (OPT).
